cot_content:
Okay, let me think about how to bake chicken wings.

The question is about whether to place the wings skin side up or down on the baking sheet. I know that when roasting or baking meat with skin, it's generally better to start with the skin side down. This helps the skin render its fat and become crispy. If you put the skin side up, the skin might dry out or not get as crispy because the moisture from the meat can prevent it from crisping up properly.

So, for crispy chicken wings, you want the skin side down on the baking sheet. The high heat (450 degrees) will help the skin crisp up as the fat renders out. Also, the cooking time of about 20 minutes sounds reasonable for wings at that temperature, as they are relatively small pieces of meat and should cook through in that time.

Therefore, option B is correct - place the wings skin side down on the baking sheet.
label: minority
